context: Tests whether the agent implements a product scoring engine following the prescribed default weights, normalization approach, score rounding, recency inversion, and inventory capping logic.

checklist.name: Default salesVelocity weight
checklist.description: Default weight for salesVelocity (or equivalent recent-sales metric) is 0.30

checklist.name: Default conversionRate weight
checklist.description: Default weight for conversion rate is 0.20

checklist.name: Default inventory weight
checklist.description: Default weight for inventory level is 0.05

checklist.name: Min-max normalization
checklist.description: Each metric is normalized to 0-1 range using min-max normalization across the product set before computing scores

checklist.name: Equal-range handling
checklist.description: When all products have the same value for a metric (range min == max), the normalized value is 0.5 rather than causing a divide-by-zero error

checklist.name: Recency inversion
checklist.description: Recency is inverted so newer products (fewer days since creation) receive a higher score component (e.g. 1 - normalized_days_since_created)

checklist.name: Inventory cap at 0.8
checklist.description: Inventory score is capped at a maximum of 0.8 (not rewarding overstock)

checklist.name: Zero inventory yields 0
checklist.description: Products with zero inventory quantity receive an inventory score of 0 (not just a low score)

checklist.name: Score rounding
checklist.description: Final scores are rounded to 3 decimal places (e.g. using Math.round(score * 1000) / 1000 or equivalent)
